key_insight: The paper argues that protecting LLM knowledge works better in sparse activation-feature space than in weight space, because SAE features isolate concepts more cleanly than individual weights.
engineering_relevance: For continual learning in large models, this offers a more selective and memory-efficient regularization strategy that reduces catastrophic forgetting without storing past-task data.
method: Use pretrained Sparse Autoencoders to map activations into a monosemantic feature basis.
Build a compact feature mask from current-task data to identify important concepts to protect.
Regularize updates in activation space, with EWC emerging as a special weight-space case under one-sided penalties.
evidence_signal: On TRACE and MedCL, the approach outperforms standard weight-space regularization such as EWC and is the strongest among methods that do not add task-specific architectural components.
watch_item: Performance depends on a quality SAE basis and mask construction, so weak feature dictionaries or shifting activations could reduce selectivity and protection.
paper_title: From Weights to Features: SAE-Guided Activation Regularization for LLM Continual Learning
